# Senior Engineering Program Manager, AI/Machine Learning- Open to Remote in US
...
Senior Engineering Program Manager, AI/Machine Learning- Open to Remote in US Location: Open to remote in US with preference to sit near Colorado or Minnesota campus Careers that Change Lives A career at Medtronic is like no other. We're purposeful. We're committed.
...
We reward you in the ways that matter most to you. We offer a wide range of benefits and rewards programs to recognize the important role you play in our Mission to transform healthcare.Learn more about Medtronic benefits and compensation at the bottom of this job description. In this exciting role as a Senior Engineering Program Manager AI/ML, you will have responsibility for leading Generative Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning programs within the Global Operations Quality Solutions Organization.
...
The Sr. Program Manager is accountable for ensuring software execution and delivery across agile/scrum teams. This role requires a combination of technical expertise, program management skills, and domain knowledge in artificial intelligence and machine learning.
...
A Day in the Life Responsibilities include but are not limited to: • Responsible for project planning including identifying requirements, developing work plans, managing program and project budgets, building schedules, and partnering with team members to determine and communicate functional and/or resource requirements. • Own and Manage execution against an aggressive but achievable program and/or project plan, provide work direction to team members, work with peer project managers/cross functional program team members to monitor dependencies and ensure that critical program milestones and deliverables are met. • Collaborate with Core Team Leaders, Architects & Systems Engineers, and Human Factors Engineers to drive projects within scope, arenaflex, and schedule. • Lead a team of technical and software engineers (Employees and Vendor resources) to deliver program commitments. • Communicate project status and issues to functional and core team leadership as appropriate. Proactively manage project quality, risks, and issues. • Provide input for setting team member's performance objectives and provide feedback to the functional leaders about performance against project deliverables and core competencies. Must Have: Minimum Requirements • Bachelors degree required • Minimum of 7 years of relevant Computer Science/Software Engineering experience experience, or advanced degree with a minimum of 5 years relevant Computer Science/Software Engineering experience experience Nice to Have • Bachelor's degree in a technical discipline such as Computer Science, Software Engineering, Math, Statistics or Data Science. • Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, or other languages/libraries/platforms and tools commonly used in AI/ML development. • Strong understanding of fundamental computer science concepts, software design best practices and software development lifecycle. • Solid understanding of foundational machine learning concepts.

highlights:

# Principal Advanced analytics Analyst
...
Title: Principal Adv Analytics Analyst
...
Job Description:
...
- At Medtronic you can begin a life-long career of exploration and innovation, while helping champion healthcare access and equity for all. You’ll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation in a more connected, compassionate world.
- A Day in
...
Enterprise Operations Insights (EOI) - Subject Matter Expert and Scrum Product Owner For GOSC Finance
...
At Medtronic, we bring bold ideas forward with speed and decisiveness to put patients first in everything we do. This position is remote to enhance our competitive edge and expand our cross-functional collaboration efforts.
...
Primarily they are responsible for building insights and analytics while providing subject matter expertise, technical knowledge, and analytic product ownership. They will create user workflows, analytics, and insights to bring them to life.
...
The role leads an existing team (squad) within Enterprise Operations Insights (EOI) dedicated to the GOSC Finance function within Medtronic. The mission of this role is to contribute to the building of INSIGHTS to drive best-in-class practices and tools within GOSC Finance.
...
- The role will also need to work hand in hand with other departments within Enterprise Operations Insights (EOI), including running daily scrum meetings with the Analytics Development Team and the Data Engineering Team to manage the creation and deployment of solutions that simplify our customers processes and generate insights that align with our customers goals. This role requires you to be the user of your tools and generate insights to share with the business.
...
- Furthermore, the role needs to seek best practices, educate our stakeholders, and be an agent of change within the organization to educate and drive data-based decisions and workflows for our stakeholders.
...
- Fully remote for the right candidate, but we prefer candidates located near any Medtronic facility. Current team members are located near Medtronic facilities in Minneapolis, Memphis, Jacksonville, Canada, and Ireland.
...
- Responsibilities:
...
- Facilitate the ideation and creation of analytical solutions for various functions.
- Capture requirements and suggest improvements for analytical processes.
- Collaborate extensively across cross-functional teams and the organization.
- Drive the development and deployment of analytical solutions, fostering analytics and insights that yield positive business outcomes.
- Ensure the development of Analytics & Insights aligns with the End-to-End (E2E) business processes and business objectives.
...
- Act as a key resource to support and drive analytics initiatives.
- Ensure a high level of customer value and satisfaction through extreme ownership.
- Promote the adoption and usage of analytics tools and insights.
- Support training of analytic tools.
- Ensure timely and efficient delivery of solutions.
- Serve as a steward of your analytics by generating insights for business use cases.
...
### Qualifications:
...
- Must Have (Minimum Requirements) :
- Bachelor’s degree required.

- Nice to Have
- Requires a University Degree and minimum of 7 years of relevant experience in Finance Analytics with past responsibilities as a Product Owner in a Scrum environment.
- Ability to influence decisions and drive projects to completion.
- SQL, Snowflake, and Oracle data model experience
- Visualization experience such as PowerBI
- PowerApps and Power Automate experience.
- Experience working with Agile tools such as DevOps
...
- We are engineers at heart— putting ambitious ideas to work to generate real solutions for real people. From the R&D lab, to the factory floor, to the conference room, every one of us experiments, creates, builds, improves and solves. We have the talent, diverse perspectives, and guts to engineer the extraordinary.

results.title: Sr Clinical IT Developer- SAS Programming | Jobflarely
highlights: tronic you can begin a life-long career of exploration and innovation, while helping champion healthcare access and equity for all. You’ll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation in a more connected, compassionate world. A Day in the Life In this exciting role as the statistical programmer, you will be responsible for providing experienced, comprehensive, and advanced technical expertise as part of the centralized Biostats and Clinical Intelligence (BCI) team in the Medtronic Clinical & Regulatory Solutions organization (MCRS). You will be developing and maintaining programs and process methodology to meet internal and external clients’ needs. You will be planning and leading the development of solutions to all statistical analysis programming needs, particularly complex studies such as Premarket Approval (PMAs) studies. Join a diverse team of innovators who bring their worldview, their unique backgrounds, and their individual life experiences to work every day. It’s no accident — we work hard to cultivate a workforce that reflects our patients and partners. We believe it’s the only way to drive healthcare forward and remain a global leader in medical technology and solutions. MCRS provides end-to-end clinical and scientific solutions for Medtronic including: • Clinical Operations: Study Administration, Data Solutions, Monitoring, Clinical Safety, Study Solutions • Evidence Generation: Surveillance Solutions (PACS), Scientific Solutions • Operational Excellence • Strategic Account Management • Operations Support and Analytics Responsibilities may include the following and other duties may be assigned. Designs, develops, evaluates and modifies computer programs to analyze and evaluate clinical data. Recognizes inconsistencies and initiates resolution of data problems. Implements data management plans designed to meet project and protocol deadlines, provides expertise in the design and development of clinical trials, protocols and case report forms. Plans and coordinates statistical programming for complex studies. May integrate databases from multiple studies or sources. Reviews Statistical Analysis Plans from the programming perspective. Assists in data cleaning process. Programs analysis database manipulations and transfers of data for internal and external clients. Programs, tests, documents, and validate statistical programs and tools in accordance with SP standards and validation procedures. Serves as SP support programmer representing Statistical Programming on the corporate team. May serve as SP Lead in the absence of Lead Programmer. Develops and maintains good communications and working relationships with teams and external clients. May interact with other SP team members and cross functional teams to negotiate timelines, responsibilities, and deliverables; may serve as Project Manager or technical liaison with other departments; may participate in relevant client presentations and proposal development. Assists in the development of training materials for department. Understands and complies with Standard Operating Procedures and Work Instructions. Assists in developing, revising, and maintaining Standard Operating Procedures and Work Instructions. Provides advanced technical expertise in conjunction with internal and external clients. Assists in collaboration with data management team in developing and implementing new technologies or software Independently brings project solutions to SP teams. May Oversee the work of outsourcing partners and vendors at study level Mentors junior programming in departmental procedures and developing technical and/or database expertise. Meets milestones as assigned and performs other duties as directed by SP management. Meets milestones as assigned and performs other duties as directed by SP management. Must Have (Minimum Requirements): To be considered for this role, the minimum requirements must be evident on your resume. Bachelor’s degree and 2

results.title: Nicolas Anken
highlights:
Sr. Data Science Engineer EMEA at [Medtronic](https://www.linkedin.com/company/medtronic)
...
I am an experienced Analyst whose skills are linked to Data Science, Operations Research, Simulation and Business Intelligence (BI). I am interested in implementing decision support tools with concrete applications in the Logistics, Production, or Supply Chain sectors: whether to improve the efficiency of production flows, optimize storage areas, or rationalize the allocation of human or machine resources, I design tools to support facts-based decision-making. My methodology is based on the DMAIC approach and process/flow mapping. My technical skills include programming, data analysis and DataViz. My distinctive strength for decision support are algorithmic toolset, such as mathematical modeling, AI heuristics and simulation softwares. With more than 15 years of experience, I have been exposed to the main tools on the market and I have led benchmarking and purchasing processes for a BI tool, a simulation software, and a mathematical solver. I have evolved in the health sectors, the connector industry and the medical devices industry, and held positions reporting to IT, Finance, Supply Chain, OPEX or Engineering. This diversity has given me a broad understanding of the needs within a company and I am thus able to collaborate effectively with people at all levels and from all functions. My internal clients appreciate my agreeable team player attitude, my customer-centric orientation, and my methodical approach. I am also acknowledged for my swift comprehension of diverse requests and my proficiency in quickly and efficiently resolving technical problems. I am autonomous in my area of expertise, and I also enjoy sharing my knowledge by supervising interns and leading workshops with Super Users. I have a Master's degree in Applied Mathematics from EPFL, a CAS in Supply Chain Analytics, and am also GreenBelt certified. Curious and eager to learn, I followed several continuing training courses in financial and cost accounting, LEAN management, ITIL best practices, and also in interpersonal skills: communication, positive feedback, self-knowledge. Finally, I enjoy participating in conferences to present my technical work in an accessible manner and to engage with both expert and novice audiences. *** Operations Research, aka Prescriptive Analytics *** - Mathematical Modeling and solvers (Gurobi, CBC, CPLEX, AMPL) - Simulation / Digital Twins (Simul8, Arena, ProModel, FlexSim) - AI Meta-Heuristics (e.g Tabu, Genetic Algorithm) - Programming (Python, R, SQL, Matlab, VBA, PERL, C++) - DataViz BI tools (Spotfire, Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
...
#### Sr. Data Science Engineer EMEA (Current)
...
Joined the Operations Innovation – Digital Transformation Team, while continuing to work and collaborate (dotted-line) within the Horizontal Deployment Engine for the EMEA Region. My responsibilities include leading Advanced Analytics initiatives, such as Prescriptive Analytics applications development, Simulation/Digital Twin projects, AI-driven solutions, and multi-sites reporting.
...
- Applying OR within an AI tool to support the digitalization project for Site Evolution Analysis: production capacity modeling using linear programming (using the pyomo Python package), taking production cycle times and components supply as constraints.
...
- Leading the benchmarking and purchasing process of a commercial mathematical solver.
...
- Assessing the opportunity to implement Simulation/Digital Twin tools at our production site

results.title: Medtronic Data Scientist Interview Questions & Guide 2026
highlights:
### What is a Data Scientist at Medtronic?
...
As a **Data Scientist** at **Medtronic**, you will operate at the intersection of advanced analytics, machine learning, and clinical science. Your primary objective is to transform complex clinical trials, registries, and real-world data sources—such as electronic health records (EHR) and insurance claims—into actionable strategic insights. These insights directly influence clinical methodologies, regulatory submissions, and market strategies for life-saving medical devices.
...
In this role, you are not just building models in isolation; you are an embedded expert partnering with cross-functional teams. You will work alongside statisticians, clinical researchers, and product engineers to design predictive frameworks and build analytical workflows. Whether you are optimizing therapies in divisions like **Neuromodulation and Pelvic Health (NMPH)** or exploring cutting-edge methodologies like synthetic data and digital twins, your work directly supports the core **Medtronic** mission: to alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life.
...
### Technical & Machine Learning
...
This category evaluates your understanding of machine learning theory, model selection, and practical implementation details.
...
* What are the differences between generative and discriminative models, and when would you use each?
...
* How do you integrate unit testing into your machine learning pipelines to ensure model reproducibility?
...
2Handle Highly
...
to Non-Technical Stake
...
**Role-Related Knowledge** – You must demonstrate a strong command of Python or R, SQL, and machine learning libraries. Beyond writing code, you need to show that you understand the mathematical foundations of your models and how to validate them rigorously in a regulated environment.
...
statistical rigor to clinical
...
Medtronic** relies heavily on clinical trials and
...
evidence, meaning your models must be statistically sound, interpretable, and highly validated.
...
* **Advanced concepts (less common)** – Synthetic data generation, digital twin modeling, and Bayesian inference in clinical trials.
...
Machine Learning (
...
As a **Data Scientist** at **Medtronic**, your daily work will span the entire lifecycle of data product development, from initial data discovery to model deployment and stakeholder presentation.
...
* **Data Transformation & Modeling** – You will explore, clean, and transform messy, multi-source clinical data. You will build predictive models, classification algorithms, and forecasting tools using state-of-the-art statistical and machine learning methodologies.
...
clinical trial design

regulatory submissions,
...
market positioning.
...
### Role Requirements & Qualifications
...
* **Must-have technical skills** – Expert-level proficiency in Python or R, strong SQL capabilities, and deep knowledge of machine learning frameworks and inferential statistics.
...
* **Must-have experience** – A Bachelor's degree with a minimum of 10 years of experience in data analytics, or an advanced degree (Master's or Ph.D.) in a quantitative field (e.g., Data Science, Statistics, Biostatistics, Computer Science) with a minimum of 8 years of experience.
...
* **Nice-to-have skills** – Experience working within the medical device, healthcare, or pharmaceutical industries. Familiarity with synthetic data, digital twins, and clinical trial regulations is highly valued.
...
Functional Questions**

"so what."

It is a career path where your analytical insights translate directly into improved patient therapies, safer medical devices, and more efficient healthcare delivery.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore

# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.

machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling

Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities

Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.
